task.title: Deleting a file on a remote system
task.shortdesc: You can delete a file in the Remote Systems view or the view.

context.p: Deleting a resource deletes it from the remote system. If you want only to remove a resource from a subproject, use the Remove from Subproject action in the view. These two actions have similar names, but their function is different. The Delete action deletes the file from the remote system. The Remove from Subproject action deletes the file from the subproject, but not from the remote system. The file remains on the remote system and can still be edited or added to another subproject.

A third action is also similarly named, but different in function: Remove from Retrieved Data Sets. This action removes a file from the Retrieved Data Sets filter, but not from the remote system. The file remains on the remote system and can still be edited or added to a subproject.
context.note: System administrators can disable the Delete action on the Remote Systems and views. For instructions on disabling this action, refer to the Host Configuration Guide (SC23-76585).

step.cmd: In the Remote Systems view or the z/OS Projects view, right-click the resource that you want to delete and click Delete.
When you are prompted to confirm the deletion, click Delete. The resource is deleted.
step.info: When you delete a generation data group, all generation data sets in the GDG are deleted and then the GDG base is deleted.
